Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log with the following columns Case ID Timestamp Activity Resource Region Complexity The goal is to identify cases with performance issues and analyze the attributes Resource Region Complexity to determine root causes of extended durations

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Calculate the total duration for each case from the first event to the last event Identify cases that take significantly longer We can set a threshold based on the mean plus 2 times std or by visual inspection or compare with the majority

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets compute the duration for each case Case 2001 Duration 1 point 5 hours Case 2002 Duration 25 hours 55 minutes approx 25 point 92 hours Case 2003 Duration 48 hours 20 minutes approx 48 point 33 hours Case 2004 Duration 1 point 42 hours Case 2005 Duration 77 hours 5 minutes approx 77 point 08 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The cases that are taking significantly longer are 2002 2003 and 2005 However note that 2001 and 2004 are very fast

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity Low Cases 2001 and 2004 both fast Medium Case 2002 longer High Cases 2003 and 2005 much longer So complexity seems to be a factor medium and high complexity cases take longer

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In high complexity cases 2003 and 2005 we see multiple Request Additional Documents events and long gaps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now lets look at the resources and regions Region A Case 2001 Low fast Case 2003 High slow Region B Case 2002 Medium slow Case 2004 Low fast Case 2005 High very slow

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Both regions have fast and slow cases but the slow cases are associated with higher complexity However within high complexity Region B is slower

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Adjuster Lisa is handling Case 2002 and Case 2005 in Region B both are slow Adjuster Mike is handling Case 2001 fast and Case 2003 slow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For Case 2002 the delay seems to be between Evaluate Claim and Request Additional Documents gap 4 hours 15 minutes and then a night and day until next day Approve Claim For Case 2003 there are repeated requests and long gaps For Case 2005 there are repeated requests and long gaps including about 22 hours and then 19 hours until approval

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We see that the delays occur mainly in the Request Additional Documents phase and the subsequent waiting until the next step

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Number of Request Additional Documents events per case Case 2001 zero Case 2002 one Case 2003 two Case 2005 three

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: More requests generally lead to longer duration because of the gaps Case 2005 three requests 77 hours Case 2003 two requests 48 hours Case 2002 one request 26 hours low complexity zero requests about 1 point 5 hours

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The approval step after the last request is consistently taking about 19 to 23 hours which might be due to a batch process or manager availability

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The long wait after requests could be because the adjuster is waiting for the customer to send the documents or because the adjuster is overloaded

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the root cause might be that the process for medium and high complexity cases requires additional steps requests and then the approval step is taking a long time after the last request

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Adjuster Lisa might be overburdened because she handled two slow cases But note those cases were also medium and high complexity

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: If there were better initial evaluation or a checklist for high complexity cases they might request all necessary documents at once and reduce repeated requests

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Proposed root causes Complexity level is a major factor high complexity claims take longer because they require more requests for additional documents and more time for approval The number of requests per case is directly related to the total duration The approval step after the last request is taking about a day

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Why do high complexity claims take longer They require more in depth evaluation which may lead to multiple requests for additional documents The customer may take longer to respond to requests for high complexity claims

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Suggestions for mitigation Provide better guidelines or a checklist for high complexity cases to request all necessary documents at once Increase approval frequency or allow real time approval Send automated reminders to customers Check workload and training for adjusters especially in Region B

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Summary of findings Cases with performance issues 2002 2003 2005 Root causes Complexity medium and high complexity cases take longer The number of requests adds waiting time The approval step after the last request takes about a day Region B had the longest high complexity case due to three requests and long gaps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The above analysis is based on a very small dataset only 5 cases In reality we would need more data to draw statistically significant conclusions
